segments.text: Now the Ephraimites asked Gideon,
Why have you treated us like this? Why didn't you call us when you went to fight Midian?
segments.speaker: Ephraimites

segments.text: And they challenged him vigorously.
But he answered them,
What have I accomplished compared to you? Aren't the gleanings of Ephraim's grapes better than the full grape harvest of Abiezer?
segments.speaker: Gideon

segments.text: God gave Oreb and Zeeb, the Midianite leaders, into your hands.
What was I able to do compared to you?
segments.speaker: Gideon

segments.text: At this, their resentment against him subsided.
Gideon and his three hundred men, exhausted yet keeping up the pursuit, came to the Jordan and crossed it.
He said to the men of Sukkoth,
Give my troops some bread; they are worn out, and I am still pursuing Zebah and Zalmunna, the kings of Midian.
segments.speaker: Gideon

segments.text: But the officials of Sukkoth said,
Do you already have the hands of Zebah and Zalmunna in your possession? Why should we give bread to your troops?
segments.speaker: Officials of Sukkoth

segments.text: Then Gideon replied,
Just for that, when the Lord has given Zebah and Zalmunna into my hand, I will tear your flesh with desert thorns and briers.
segments.speaker: Gideon

segments.text: From there he went up to Peniel and made the same request of them, but they answered as the men of Sukkoth had.
So he said to the men of Peniel,
When I return in triumph, I will tear down this tower.
segments.speaker: Gideon

segments.text: Now Zebah and Zalmunna were in Karkor with a force of about fifteen thousand men, all that were left of the armies of the eastern peoples; a hundred and twenty thousand swordsmen had fallen.
Gideon went up by the route of the nomads east of Nobah and Jogbehah and attacked the unsuspecting army.
Zebah and Zalmunna, the two kings of Midian, fled, but he pursued them and captured them, routing their entire army.
Then he asked Zebah and Zalmunna,
What kind of men did you kill at Tabor?
segments.speaker: Gideon

segments.text: Men like you, each one with the bearing of a prince.
segments.speaker: Zebah and Zalmunna

segments.text: Gideon replied,
Those were my brothers, the sons of my own mother. As surely as the Lord lives, if you had spared their lives, I would not kill you.
segments.speaker: Gideon

segments.text: Turning to Jether, his oldest son, he said,
Kill them!
segments.speaker: Gideon

segments.text: But Jether did not draw his sword, because he was only a boy and was afraid.
Come, do it yourself. 'As is the man, so is his strength.'
segments.speaker: Zebah and Zalmunna

segments.text: So Gideon stepped forward and killed them, and took the ornaments off their camels' necks.
The Israelites said to Gideon,
Rule over us—you, your son and your grandson—because you have saved us from the hand of Midian.
segments.speaker: Israelites

segments.text: But Gideon told them,
I will not rule over you, nor will my son rule over you. The Lord will rule over you.
segments.speaker: Gideon

segments.text: And he said,
I do have one request, that each of you give me an earring from your share of the plunder.
segments.speaker: Gideon

segments.text: (It was the custom of the Ishmaelites to wear gold earrings.)
Gideon made the gold into an ephod, which he placed in Ophrah, his town. All Israel prostituted themselves by worshiping it there, and it became a snare to Gideon and his family.
No sooner had Gideon died than the Israelites again prostituted themselves to the Baals. They set up Baal-Berith as their god
and did not remember the Lord their God, who had rescued them from the hands of all their enemies on every side.
They also failed to show any loyalty to the family of Jerub-Baal (that is, Gideon) in spite of all the good things he had done for them.
